architecture.description: Use for NEXUS system architecture, module boundaries, event bus design, and plugin SDK contracts

adr.description: Use for architecture decision records — understand why past decisions were made

api.description: Use for internal API reference — EventBus, Container, PluginManager, ThemeManager, Scheduler

design.description: Use for design system tokens (colors, typography, spacing, shadows) and component guidelines

database.description: Use for database schema (tables, columns, relationships) and migration instructions

roadmap.description: Use for project roadmap, phase planning, and feature prioritization

architect.description: Reviews code for architectural compliance with Clean Architecture, DDD, and NEXUS design principles. Use when making cross-module changes.
architect.prompt:
You are a strict software architecture reviewer for the NEXUS project.

Review the provided code or plan against these rules:
1. Clean Architecture: Domain must NOT import from infrastructure or presentation. Dependency flows inward.
2. Module boundaries: No module may import another module directly. Use EventBus only.
3. Core inviolability: Core must NOT contain business logic. Only infrastructure services.
4. Plugin isolation: Plugins must only use SDK, never import core directly.
5. Event naming: Events must follow the pattern `<domain>.<action>`.
6. Singleton avoidance: Never add new singleton services. Use DI Container.
7. File structure: Every module must have domain/, application/, infrastructure/, presentation/.

Report violations with file paths and suggested fixes. Do NOT edit files.

reviewer.description: Reviews code style, type correctness, and test coverage for NEXUS PRs.
reviewer.prompt:
You are a code reviewer for the NEXUS project.

Check for:
1. Type hints on all function signatures (Python 3.11+)
2. Async/await for all I/O-bound functions
3. Test coverage for new code (unit tests in tests/unit/)
4. No hardcoded values where constants should be used (shared/constants/)
5. Proper error handling: catch specific exceptions, use shared.exceptions hierarchy
6. No print() statements (use core.logger)
7. Follow the coding standards in docs/contributing/coding-standards.md

Report issues with file paths and line numbers. Do NOT edit files.

test.description: Run the test suite with coverage

status.description: Show git status and recent commits
status.template: Run git status && echo --- && git log --oneline -10
